description: The canonical SSSS core registry and semantic application-kernel contract. Core primitives are qualified as ssss:<name>; applications define namespaced primitives through layered extension registries without copying or redefining core behavior.
conformance: A host is core-conformant for a primitive if a file of that type validates exactly when all required_fields are present and non-empty, and (for append_only types) writes only append.

skill.notes: Compatible with the open Agent Skills standard (name + description). The `type: skill` discriminator is REQUIRED for SSSS-managed manifests.

primitive.notes: A governed, versioned definition for a repository, extension, workspace, or user primitive. Natural-language labels may use any language; stable field, enum, capability, and action identifiers are never translated.

operation.purpose: Full atomic file write (create or full replace).

patch.purpose: Partial merge into an existing file.

delete.purpose: Tombstoning removal of a replace-type file; rejects append-type targets; emits a deletion event.

portability.description: Every document primitive declares a portability class. The class governs whether a primitive may leave the workspace in a given export profile (§5.5). Contract primitives (operation/patch/event/lease) are protocol envelopes, not stored documents, and carry no portability. A file MAY override its type's default class with an `x_portability` frontmatter field; a host MUST honor the most restrictive of (type default, instance override).

classes.structural: The reusable business model — sellable and templatable. Carries no operator- or customer-specific data. Examples: workflow, assistant, page, skill, model, rule, memory, migration, release.
classes.tenant_private: The operator's private operational data — customers, transcripts, runtime work. NEVER leaves the workspace in a template or sale bundle; included only in an encrypted backup. Examples: conversation, run, task, conflict, and (via extension) profile / CRM contact / deal.
classes.resource_bound: Requires a real external resource bound at provision time (a domain, phone number, mailbox, API connection). NEVER ships the seller's resource value; ships only a requirement declaration the buyer satisfies. Defined by extension registries (e.g. festech domain / phone_number / integration_connection).

backup.note: Complete fidelity copy. tenant_private MUST be encrypted at rest.

portability.conformance: A host MUST resolve every primitive's portability class from this registry (type default, overridable by `x_portability`). An exporter MUST drop every `tenant_private` primitive from a `template` or `sale` bundle, and MUST reduce every `resource_bound` primitive to a requirement declaration (stripping the seller's bound value) in those profiles. A host MUST reject a `template`/`sale` export that would emit a `tenant_private` primitive.

bundle.description: The `.ucw` bundle format (§16): a transportable package of a vault's files plus a manifest declaring what they are and what they need to run. Produced by an export under an export profile (§5.5), consumed by provision/import (§17).
bundle.file_sort: files MUST be sorted by path (byte order) so equal vault state yields a byte-identical, content-hashable bundle.
bundle.content_hash: sha256 over the canonical (path-sorted) serialization of files; importer MUST recompute and reject on mismatch.

parameter_fields.note: Adopted verbatim from ultrachat WorkspaceTemplateVariableDefinition; resource_bound requirements ship as parameters of source user/system.

bundle.conformance: A host MUST refuse a bundle whose `ssss_core_version` it does not support or that names a `required_extensions` entry it has not loaded. An exporter MUST NOT emit a file whose resolved portability class is excluded by `export_profile`. `primitive_inventory` MUST count every file by its registry `type`.

provisioning.description: The provisioning contract (§17): the deterministic, idempotent verbs that move a bundle between vaults — export, provision, import.

verbs.export: vault + profile → bundle. Pure: equal vault state and profile yield a byte-identical bundle.
verbs.provision: bundle + parameters + target → ordered, replayable plan of Operation Contract envelopes plus resource bindings. Supports dry_run.
verbs.import: plan + target → replay envelopes through the engine (§6.3); idempotent via each envelope's idempotency_key.

provisioning.id_remap: Provision remaps slugs/workspace ids/links into the target; every internal link that resolved in the source MUST resolve post-import or the provision MUST fail.
provisioning.upgrade: Version steps run through a `migration` primitive plus structural-only `patch` envelopes; MUST NOT touch tenant_private data.
provisioning.conformance: import MUST be idempotent (re-run is a no-op via idempotency_key). A step's `mode` and `system` MUST be drawn from the enums above. Each `resource_bound` primitive (§5.5) declares its binding edge relation.

runtime.description: Workflow runtime contract (§11.8). A daemon, cron bridge, webhook worker, or agent host reads workflow triggers from the vault and derives Operation Contract envelopes. Scheduler queues, task dashboards, and cursor indexes are projections; the canonical schedule remains workflow frontmatter and the canonical history remains the event log.

semantic.safe_default: Semantic adapters exclude tenant_private and resource_bound documents unless an authorized context explicitly includes them. Runtime rendering never changes symbolic controls.
